name: Young Earth Drake
description: A wingless dragon-like creature whose scales resemble dirt clods that occasionally crackle with static electricity.

features.name: Tough Hide
features.description: When the Drake takes physical damage, reduce it by 3.

features.name: Chomp
features.description: Make a standard attack against a target within Melee range. On a success, spend a Fear to deal an additional 1d12 physical damage and the target must mark an Armor Slot without receiving its benefits (they can still use armor to reduce the damage). If they can't mark an Armor Slot, they must mark an additional HP and you gain a Fear.

features.name: Stomp
features.description: Spend a Fear to slam the ground with the Drake's powerful legs, creating a shockwave. All targets within Close range must make a Strength or Agility Reaction Roll. On a failure, they take 1d10 magic damage and are Vulnerable until they clear a Stress. On a success they take half damage.

features.name: Dug In
features.description: If the Drake is hit by an attack or ability that would force it to move, you can mark 2 Stress to remain in position.
features.trigger: hit by attack/ability that forces movement

name: Sand Sprite
description: A small, fairy-like creature made of sand and dirt.

features.name: Flying
features.description: While flying, the Sand Sprite gains a +2 bonus to their Difficulty.

features.name: Spray Sand
features.description: Spend a Fear to cover all targets within Close range with fine sand. Targets must make an Agility or Instinct Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target takes 1d6+4 physical damage, marks a Stress, and is Vulnerable until they clear a Stress. On a success, they take half damage and have disadvantage on their next Action Roll.

features.name: Evade
features.description: When a PC fails a roll targeting the Sprite, it can move to a point within Close range.
features.trigger: PC fails a roll targeting the Sprite

name: Minor Earth Elemental
description: A collection of rocks and stones that move as a cohesive unit.

features.name: Like a Rock
features.description: Before they make their first attack in a fight or after they become Hidden, the Elemental is indistinguishable from other rocks until their next attack or a PC succeeds on an Instinct Roll to identify them.

features.name: Rolling Thunder
features.description: Spend a Fear to roll and tumble in a straight line to a point in Far range. Every PC within Melee range of the line must make an Agility Reaction Roll. On a failure, they take 1d12+4 physical damage and mark a Stress. On a success, they take half damage.

features.name: Surprise Slam
features.description: If a PC moves within Very Close range of the Elemental while it is Hidden, it can move and make a standard attack with advantage against that PC.
features.trigger: PC moves within Very Close range while Hidden

name: Sparkflies
description: A swarm of sparkling insects who consume electricity.

features.name: Horde (1d6+1)
features.description: When the Sparkflies have marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals 1d6+1 magic damage instead.

features.name: Flying
features.description: While flying, the Sparkflies gain a +2 bonus to their Difficulty.

features.name: Crackle
features.description: Spend a Fear to expel electricity within Close range. All targets in the area must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or take 1d6+1 magic damage and mark a Stress.

name: Amphiptere
description: A sleek, snake-like reptile with feathered wings and a draconic face.

features.name: Minion (3)
features.description: The Amphiptere is defeated when they take any damage. For every 3 damage a PC deals to the Amphiptere, defeat an additional Minion within range the attack would succeed against.

features.name: Discouraging Bites
features.description: A PC that marks HP from an Amphiptere's "Group Attack" loses a Hope.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Spend a Fear to choose a target and spotlight all Amphiptere within Close range of them. Those Minions move into Melee range of the target and make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 2 physical damage each. Combine this damage.

name: Ashkadavar
description: A wizard's familiar, created from the bodies of several woodland creatures.

features.name: Magical Hunger
features.description: Spellcast rolls that target the Ashkadavar have disadvantage. If a PC makes a spellcast roll within Far range and fails, the Ashkadavar can mark a Stress to summon an identical Ashkadavar within Very Close range.

features.name: Unreal Existence
features.description: The Ashkadavar has resistance to physical damage. Additionally, it can mark a Stress to teleport to a location within Far range.

features.name: Magic Connection
features.description: Make a standard attack against a target. On a Success, spend a Fear. The Ashkadavar gains a +2 bonus to its damage for each other Ashkadavar within Very Close range of the target.

name: Ashkadavar Swarm
description: A group of replicating chimeric creations made from the bodies of several woodland creatures.

features.name: Horde (1d4+2)
features.description: When the Swarm has marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals 1d4+2 magic damage instead.

features.name: Magical Hunger
features.description: Spellcast rolls that target the Swarm have disadvantage. If a PC makes a spellcast roll within Far range and fails, the Swarm can mark a Stress and clear 1 HP.

features.name: Magic Surge
features.description: Make a standard attack against a target. On a success, spend a Fear to gain a bonus to the damage roll equal to the number of the Swarm's unmarked HP.

name: Hedge Wizard
description: A mystic who has learned the ways of magic by learning the ways of life.

features.name: Dubious Dealings
features.description: The Hedge Wizard must be offered a gift such as a fine wine or a magic trinket before speaking openly with the PCs. PCs that do not extend this hospitality have disadvantage on all social actions made against the Hedge Wizard.

features.name: Hex
features.description: Mark a Stress to force a PC to make a Knowledge Reaction Roll. On a failure, the PC must speak the truth until the end of the scene. If they knowingly tell a lie, they must mark 3 Stress.

features.name: Whispering Wanderers
features.description: If a PC threatens the Hedge Wizard with violence, spend a Fear to summon 1 Sylvan Soldier (or similar Tier 1 Standard adversary) for every 2 PCs, who appear at Close range and are immediately spotlighted.

name: Hippogriff
description: A flying creature with the forelegs and head of an eagle and the hindquarters of a horse trained to accept riders.

features.name: Mount
features.description: A creature that is riding the Hippogriff can cause the Hippogriff to mark a Stress to move to a Far range when they act.

features.name: Protective
features.description: A creature riding the Hippogriff can mark a Stress to add a +2 to their Difficulty (Or evasion if a PC) when targeted by an attack. If the attack would still be successful, the Hippogriff takes the damage instead.

name: Mad Mage
description: A wizard commanding creatures made by stitching together disparate parts.

features.name: Bait Beacon
features.description: Make a standard attack against a target. On a success, mark a Stress to make the target Vulnerable until they make a successful Finesse Roll to clear the condition.

features.name: Come My Pet!
features.description: Once per scene, summon a Manticore under the Mage's control. The Manticore appears at Close range and is immediately spotlighted.

features.name: Familiar Friend
features.description: When an ally within Close range makes an attack, the Mage can mark a Stress to give the ally advantage.

features.name: Chaotic Swarm
features.description: Consequence Countdown (5). When the Mage is in the spotlight for the first time, activate the countdown. When it triggers, an Ashkadavar Swarm appears at a point within Far range.

features.name: Well Done, My Minions!
features.description: When an ally causes a PC to mark HP, you gain a Fear.
features.trigger: ally causes PC to mark HP

name: Manticore
description: A lion with cinnabar-red fur, wings of a bat, the tail of a giant scorpion, and the wide face of a man.

features.name: Flying
features.description: While flying, the Manticore gains a +2 bonus to their Difficulty.

features.name: Poison Barbs
features.description: When the Manticore makes an attack with "Poison Sting" or "Barb Fling" you can spend a Fear to add 1d6 bonus to the damage roll and Poison the target until their next rest or they succeed on a Knowledge Roll (15). While Poisoned, the target must roll a d6 before they make an action roll. On a result of 4 or lower, they must mark a Stress.

features.name: Poison Sting
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, the target takes 1d6+4 physical damage. If they mark HP, they lose a Hope.

features.name: Barb Fling
features.description: Mark a Stress to make an attack against a target within Far range. On a success, the target takes 1d8+3 physical damage.

name: Urstrix
description: A bear with a feathered upper body and the head of a large owl.

features.name: Silent Stalker
features.description: If an Urstrix is Hidden when they make a successful standard attack against a target, they deal 1d20+3 physical damage instead of their standard damage.

features.name: Heavy Swipe
features.description: Mark a Stress to make an attack against all targets in front of the Urstrix within Very Close range. Targets the Urstrix succeeds against take 1d10+2 physical damage and are knocked back to Close range. If you instead spend a Fear to use this feature, the damage becomes direct.

features.name: Piercing Screech
features.description: When the Urstrix takes Major or greater damage, spend a Fear to let out a furious hoot. All targets within Close range must mark 1d4 Stress and the Urstrix becomes Hidden until their next attack or a PC succeeds on an Instinct Roll (14) to find them.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Urstrix makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

name: Untamed Experiment
description: A creature whose body is made through the whims of its creator. Some have the body of lions or the hindquarters of horses, others are goats that breathe fire.

features.name: Mad Creation
features.description: When the Experiment appears, put 8 tokens on this stat block. Spend these tokens to add features to the Experiment when it appears or during its spotlight. These are one time costs.

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.description: The Experiment can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Flying
features.description: The Experiment has wings that can support flight. While Flying, the Experiment gains a +2 bonus to their Difficulty.

features.name: Ophidian Charm
features.description: When a PC makes an attack against the Experiment within Melee range they must roll a d6. On a 3 or lower, the creature has disadvantage on their next attack roll and must mark a Stress.

features.name: Terrifying Roar
features.description: Once per scene, mark a Stress to roar loudly, intimidating all foes nearby. All targets within Close range must succeed on a Presence Reaction Roll. Targets who fail are Shaken until they roll with Hope. While Shaken, a target's Hope die is reduced to a d10.

features.name: Double Swipe
features.description: When the Experiment makes a standard attack, mark a Stress to make another against a different target within Melee range.

features.name: Ramming Charge
features.description: Mark a Stress to charge through a group within Close range and make an attack against all targets in the Experiment's path. Targets the Experiment succeeds against take 2d6+10 physical damage and are knocked back to Far range.

features.name: Powerful Kick
features.description: When the Experiment takes Major or greater damage, mark a Stress to make an attack against a target within Melee range. On a success, the target takes 1d12+2 physical damage and is knocked back to Far range.

features.name: Burning Breath
features.description: Countdown (Loop 1d4). When the Experiment sees a hostile target, activate the countdown. When it triggers, the Experiment breathes flames at all targets in front of them within a Very Close range. All targets in the area must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or take 1d8+3 magic damage. For each target that marks HP, you gain a Fear.

name: Blooming Cockatrice
description: A green, dragon-like creature with a moss-covered rooster's neck and bright flower petal wings.

features.name: Flying
features.description: While flying, the Cockatrice gains a +2 bonus to their Difficulty.

features.name: Eyes of Leaf and Stem
features.description: Before a PC within Close range makes an action roll that would affect the Cockatrice, they must succeed on a Presence Reaction Roll (10). On a failure, the PC marks a Stress as they begin to turn into a tree and start an Arborization Countdown (4). It ticks down when any PC in the scene rolls with Fear. When it triggers, the target is Restrained and Unconscious; they can't make action rolls, can't speak, and automatically fail all reaction rolls. All creatures that the Cockatrice has turned into trees clear both conditions when the Cockatrice is defeated.

features.name: Penetrating Peck
features.description: Make a standard attack against a target. On a success, spend a Fear to force the target to mark an Armor Slot without receiving its benefits (they can still use armor to reduce the damage). If they have an Arborization Countdown, tick it down by 1. If they can't mark an Armor Slot, they must mark an additional HP.

features.name: Baleful Crow
features.description: Spend a Fear to spotlight 1d4+1 allies. Attacks they make while spotlighted in this way have advantage but deal half damage.

features.name: Mama Hen
features.description: When a Budding Cockatrice Clutch marks HP, you can mark a Stress to fly into Melee range of the attacker and make a "Penetrating Peck" attack against them without spending a Fear.

name: Budding Cockatrice Clutch
description: A group of young dragon-like creatures with green fuzzy scales and tightly wrapped buds for wings waiting to bloom.

features.name: Horde (1d4+1)
features.description: When the Clutch have marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals 1d4+1 magic damage instead.

features.name: Thicket Sprout
features.description: When the Clutch have marked half or more of their HP, mark a Stress to create a bramble thicket around the Clutch in a Very Close range. All targets within the area must make a Finesse Reaction Roll (12). Targets who fail take 2d8 physical damage and are temporarily Restrained. Targets who succeed take half damage and are knocked out of the area. The Clutch can move through this area unimpeded and has a +3 bonus to its Difficulty while in the bramble thicket.

name: Burroska Doll
description: A wooden humanoid the size of a doll. They are dressed in leaves and have spiky growths all over their bodies.

features.name: Spine Explosion
features.description: When the Doll marks its last HP, all creatures within Close range must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 1d6 physical damage.

name: Fungril Gladeservant
description: A towering fungril dressed in what nature provides with a wooden blade arm and a network of vines which serve as fingers.

features.name: Entranced By Song
features.description: When a PC within Close range sings a lullaby they can make a Presence Roll (15) to cause the Gladeservant to fall Asleep until the Gladeservant marks HP or the GM spends a Fear to awaken them. While Asleep, the Gladeservant cannot act.

features.name: Blade Swing
features.description: Mark a Stress to make an attack against all targets in front of the Gladeservant. Targets the Gladeservant succeeds against take 1d8+3 physical damage and mark a Stress.

features.name: Grasping Vine
features.description: Spend a Fear to choose a target within Close range to make a Strength Reaction Roll. On a failure, the Gladeservant Envelops them and the target must mark 2 Stress. While Enveloped, the target is Restrained and must mark an additional Stress every time they make an action roll. When the Gladeservant takes Severe damage, falls Asleep, or is defeated, all Enveloped targets are freed and the condition is cleared.

features.name: Stone's Throw
features.description: Mark a Stress to throw a heavy object at a point within Far range. All targets within a Very Close range of that point must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 3d6 physical damage.

features.name: Rage of the Trees
features.description: When the Gladeservant takes damage from an attack within Close range, you can spend a Fear to make a standard attack against the attacker at advantage. On a success, clear a Stress.
features.trigger: takes damage from attack within Close range

name: Leafcutter Spriggant
description: An insect the size of a large dog with wooden armor plates taken from the bark of various trees.

features.name: Acidic Bite
features.description: Make a standard attack against a target. On a success, spend a Fear to do 2d6+2 physical damage and the target must mark an Armor Slot without gaining its benefit (they can still use armor to reduce the damage).

features.name: Bark Block
features.description: When the Spriggant takes physical damage, mark a Stress to reduce it by 3.

name: Sylvan Warden
description: A faerie wreathed in the bounty of the forest, wielding a wooden shield and gnarled staff.

features.name: Fire Flies
features.description: Spend a Fear and choose a point within Far range. A Close area around that point is swarmed by glowing insects. All targets within the area must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail glow brightly, can't become Hidden and are Vulnerable until they make a Finesse Roll to clear the condition.

features.name: Dance of Memories
features.description: Spend a Fear to call upon the forest's fae nature. All targets within Far range must succeed on a Presence Reaction Roll. Targets who fail become Forgetful until they succeed on a Knowledge Roll (10). While Forgetful, a target can't use their Experiences and have disadvantage on attacks.

features.name: Evasive Flight
features.description: When the Warden is attacked, mark a Stress to give them a +2 bonus to their Thresholds.

name: Prickleboar
description: A carnivorous boar-like creature grown from a plant; it has thorny horns and a tough, watermelon colored hide.

features.name: Heightened Senses
features.description: A PC who has marked HP can't become Hidden from the Prickleboar.

features.name: Bleeding Strike
features.description: Make a standard attack against a target. On a success, if the target marked HP, mark a Stress to make them Bloodied until they clear 1 HP or take a moment to bandage the wound with a Finesse Roll (10). While Bloodied, they can only move within Very Close range as their normal movement.

features.name: Thorn Charge
features.description: When the Prickleboar takes Severe damage, move them in a straight line to a point within Far range and make an attack against all targets in their path. Targets the Prickleboar succeeds against take 1d10+1 physical damage. You can spend a Fear to activate this reaction when the Prickleboar takes Major damage instead.

name: Woad Stalker
description: A spirit that has animated the detritus and plants that cover the forest floor into the shape of an animal. It has a golden glow from within.

features.name: Spirit Form
features.description: The Stalker is resistant to physical damage.

features.name: Fall Apart
features.description: The Stalker can mark a Stress to become Hidden until their next attack. Attacks made while the Stalker is Hidden have advantage.

features.name: Reclamate
features.description: Spend a Fear to rebuild parts of the Stalker's animated exterior, clearing 1 HP. The Stalker can mark a Stress to take the spotlight a second time this GM turn.

name: Animated Toy
description: A toy or other small object animated by an infusion of magic.

features.name: Minion (4)
features.description: The Toy is defeated when they take any damage. For every 4 damage a PC deals to the Toy, defeat an additional Minion within range the attack would succeed against.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Spend a Fear to choose a target and spotlight all Toys within Close range of them. Those Minions move into Melee range of the target and make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 1 physical damage each. Combine this damage.

features.name: Underfoot
features.description: When a PC makes an attack within Very Close range of a Toy, the Toy can move into Melee range and make a standard attack.
features.trigger: PC attacks within Very Close range

name: Dolliglop
description: A small, ooze-like creature that inhabits a child's doll or other toy and feeds off emotion.

features.name: Pay the Price
features.description: When a PC spends Hope within Far range of the Dolliglop, you gain a Fear.

features.name: Island of Misfits
features.description: Twice per scene, spend a Fear to summon a number of Animated Toys equal to twice the number of PCs. They appear within Far range and make a "Group Attack" against a chosen target.

features.name: Outgrown and Discarded
features.description: Spend a Fear to emanate a wave of loss and sadness from the Dolliglop. Each target within Far range must make an Instinct or Presence Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target vividly recalls a memory of a lost toy or keepsake and they have disadvantage on all action rolls until they roll with Hope. Targets who succeed mark a Stress.

features.name: Feed on Hope
features.description: If a PC rolls with Hope within Far range of the Dolliglop, you can mark a Stress and roll a d20. If the result is equal to or lower than the number on the PC's Hope die, the Dolliglop clears 1 HP and you gain a Fear.
features.trigger: PC rolls with Hope within Far range

name: Forlorn Spirit
description: A distraught or displaced soul, seeking either redemption or solace.

features.name: Ghost
features.description: The Spirit has resistance to physical damage. Mark a Stress to move up to Close range through solid objects.

features.name: Forlorn Wail
features.description: Spend a Fear to howl at all targets within Far range, forcing them to make an Instinct or Presence Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target must use a d10 for their Hope die until they roll with Hope or the scene ends. On a success, the target marks a Stress, but gleans a course of action that might put the Spirit to rest.

features.name: Feel Your Pain
features.description: When a PC marks HP within Close range of the Spirit, mark a Stress to have the Spirit immediately move into Melee range of that PC and make a standard attack.
features.trigger: PC marks HP within Close range

name: Wish Spirit
description: A primal spirit of creation drawn to the emotional power of a genuine wish, seeking to make mortals' desires come true.

features.name: Ephemeral
features.description: The Wish Spirit is resistant to physical damage.

features.name: Flickering Dream
features.description: Wish Spirits possess incredible power, but only within the realm of making mortals' wishes come true. Mark a Stress to bring a mortal's wish to life within the bounds of the current environment until the end of the scene. At the end of the scene, the Wish Spirit must mark another Stress or the wish dissipates, leaving the mortal with just the memory of its brief appearance.

features.name: Heart's Desire
features.description: The Wish Spirit reads the emotions and intentions of a creature within Close range. The target may allow the spirit to read their emotions or make a Presence Reaction Roll to conceal their desires. If they allow the spirit to read them, they gain a Hope.

features.name: Tenuous Hope
features.description: When a Wish Spirit marks its last Stress to sustain a wish, ask the player that made the wish to roll a Hope die (if the wish is especially large in scale or requires substantial changes to the lives of others, roll two Hope dice and take the lower result). If the result is higher than the current Fear count, the Wish Spirit gives up the last of its life force to make the wish permanent. If the result is equal to or lower than the current Fear count, the Wish Spirit cannot maintain its magic and the dream dissipates. All present must succeed on a Presence Reaction Roll or lose 1d4 Hope.
features.trigger: marks last Stress to sustain a wish

name: Wish Spirit Knot
description: A cluster of confused Wish Spirits, their tethers to wish tokens tangled and knotted together in a confounding muddle of contradictory wishes and desires.
motives: give people what the knot thinks they want

features.name: Horde (2d6)
features.description: When the Wish Spirit Knot has marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals 2d6 magical damage instead.

features.name: Confused Wishseekers
features.description: The Knot approaches mortals as they make wishes, eagerly adding those wish tokens to their collection and trying to satisfy multiple wishes at once.

features.name: Disentangle
features.description: A PC can attempt a roll to disentangle the Wish Spirit Knot, physically using Finesse, socially using Presence, or magically using their Spellcast Trait. On a success, replace the Wish Spirit Knot with a number of Wish Spirits equal to the Knot's unmarked HP. Each of these resulting spirits marks a Stress and the GM loses a Fear.

features.name: Ephemeral
features.description: The Wish Spirit Knot is resistant to physical damage.

features.name: Contradictory Dreams
features.description: Mark a Stress as the Knot tries to grant several contradictory wishes at once, warping reality around the Knot. All creatures within Far range must succeed on a Strength Reaction Roll or mark a Stress as reality shifts to try to accommodate mutually-exclusive dreams. If half or more of the PCs fail the roll, gain a Fear.

features.name: Shattered Hopes
features.description: When an attack succeeds against the Wish Spirit Knot, force the attacker to make an Instinct Reaction Roll. On a failure, ask the player to share one of their character's wishes, then describe how it would feel to have that wish thwarted forever and lose a Hope.

features.name: Broken Knot
features.description: When the Wish Spirit Knot marks their last Stress, ask the nearest player to roll their Hope die. If the result is higher than the current Fear count, the strands connecting the Wish Spirit Knot come loose. Replace the Knot with a number of Wish Spirits equal to the Knot's remaining unmarked HP. Each PC within Far range gains 2 Hope.

name: Demanding Crowd
description: A group of tourists from across the region, here to make wishes and be seen participating in the local custom. Demanding, incurious, and unwittingly making everything worse.

features.name: Horde (2d4)
features.description: When the Demanding Crowd has marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals 2d4 physical damage instead.

features.name: Antisocial Entitlement
features.description: The members of this crowd believe that they're owed fulfillment of their wishes. Rolls to convince them to disperse or give up on making wishes in the glade are made with disadvantage.

features.name: Heckling & Harassment
features.description: Spend a Fear to force all targets within Far range to make a Presence Reaction Roll or mark 2 Stress. If the Demanding Crowd has half or more of their HP marked or all their Stress marked, targets that fail only mark 1 Stress.

features.name: Panicked Trampling
features.description: When Demanding Crowd marks HP or Stress, they move a Far distance around the scene, trampling over any target in their way. Anyone they move past/through must succeed on a Strength Reaction Roll or be knocked over and trampled, taking 2d6 physical damage.

name: Alraune Queen
description: A walking plant covered in rose blooms and dagger-sized thorns. It grows by consuming the blood of its victims.

features.name: Watered By Blood
features.description: The Queen enters the scene with 2d4 blood tokens. For each token the Queen has they gain a +1 bonus to damage. If a target marks HP within Close range of the Queen, she gains another token. If the Queen takes Severe damage, she loses 1d4 tokens.

features.name: See How My Garden Grows
features.description: Spend a Fear to Summon 1d4 Young Alraune that appear within Close range and immediately take the spotlight.

features.name: Feed, My Children
features.description: Spend a Fear to spotlight 1d4+1 allies. Attacks they make while spotlighted in this way deal half damage.

features.name: Thorn Cage
features.description: Mark a Stress to make an attack against a target within Far range. On a success, deal 2d10 physical damage and the target must succeed on a Strength Reaction Roll or become Restrained. If the target is flying they take an additional 1d10 damage (combine the damage). The target can break free with a successful Strength or Finesse Roll.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Queen makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

name: Young Alraune
description: An immature alraune that hides in the dirt, only exposing its large flowering head when it is ready to strike.

features.name: Confusing Scent
features.description: Before a PC within Very Close range would make an action roll, they must succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll or mark a Stress.

features.name: Pack Tactics
features.description: If the Alraune makes a successful standard attack and another Young Alraune is within Melee range of the target, deal 2d10+4 physical damage instead of their standard damage and you gain a Fear.

features.name: Burrow
features.description: If an attack misses the Alraune, you can mark a Stress to become Hidden until the Alraune's next attack or a PC succeeds on an Instinct Roll (14) to find them.

name: Boggart
description: An apparition that haunts homes with violent pranks and theft of possessions.

features.name: Harmed By ...
features.description: The Boggart is immune to physical and magical damage unless they are made Vulnerable. Only a specific material can damage them as determined by the GM. Some examples might be salt, iron, fire, or appeasement. In such cases, the Boggart marks all Stress and becomes Vulnerable.

features.name: Get Out!
features.description: Mark a Stress to make an attack against all targets within Very Close range. Targets the Boggart succeeds against take no damage, but become Vulnerable until the end of the scene.

name: Bunyip
description: A frog-like creature that stands three stories tall. Its immense jaws can swallow a person whole.

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.description: The Bunyip can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Bellowing Roar
features.description: Spend 2 Fear to let out a reverberating call. All targets in the scene must succeed on a Strength or Instinct Reaction Roll. Targets who fail become Hopeless. While Hopeless a target cannot gain Hope until the Bunyip is defeated.

features.name: Tentacle Grab
features.description: Make a standard attack against a target. On a success, mark a Stress to pull the target into Melee range of the Bunyip. The target is temporarily Restrained.

features.name: Tentacle Swing
features.description: Mark a Stress to make an attack against all targets in front of the Bunyip at Very Close range. Targets the Bunyip succeeds against take 2d8+6 physical damage and you gain a Fear for each.

features.name: Devour
features.description: Force a target the Bunyip has Restrained to make a Strength Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target takes 2d8 direct physical damage and is Devoured. While Devoured, the target is also Hidden and must mark a Stress to make an action roll. When the Bunyip takes Severe damage, all Devoured targets are freed and the condition is cleared.

features.name: Digest
features.description: Spend a Fear to cause all Devoured targets to take 2d8 direct physical damage.

name: Dullahan
description: A headless centaur in fae armor with a flaming spine whip who is trailed by the flying heads of those it has killed.

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.description: The Dullahan can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Geis
features.description: Choose a target within Far range to make a Presence Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target is Cursed until the Dullahan is defeated. While Cursed, the target's Fear die is a d20. On a roll with Fear, the target loses a Hope.

features.name: Death's Head
features.description: Spend a Fear to make an attack against three targets within Far range. If the target is Cursed, the Dullahan has advantage. On a success, the target takes 2d6+10 magic damage.

features.name: Nightshod Hooves
features.description: Mark a Stress to make an attack against all targets behind the Dullahan within Very Close range. Targets the Dullahan succeeds against take 2d20 physical damage and are knocked back to Far range.

features.name: Stag Charge
features.description: Countdown (Loop 1d6). When the Dullahan is in the spotlight for the first time, activate the countdown. When it triggers, move the Dullahan in a straight line to a point within Far range. All targets within its path must make an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 2d8+4 magic damage. Targets who succeed take half damage.

name: Lindwyrm
description: A lanky reptile with green scales and avian legs that crawls through the canopies, dropping down on unsuspecting prey.

features.name: Forest Climber
features.description: The Lindwyrm climbs just as easily as they run. When attacking from above, the Lindwyrm has advantage.

features.name: Savage Slam
features.description: Spend a Fear and target a group within Close range. All targets must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 2d6+10 physical damage must mark a stress. All targets are knocked back to Far range.

features.name: Entangling Tail
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Close range. On a success, the target takes 2d8+3 physical damage and is Restrained. While Restrained the target moves with the Lindwyrm and each time the Lindwyrm takes an action, the target must mark a Stress. A target can break free with a successful Strength Roll.

name: Mandrake Knot
description: A group of mandrake roots tied together by their stems. They howl and scream, causing death in those that hear them.

features.name: Horde (2d6+2)
features.description: When the Mandrake Knot has marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals 2d6+2 physical damage instead.

features.name: Howl of Impending Doom
features.description: Spend a Fear to cause the Mandrake Knot to scream. All targets within Very Close range of the Mandrake Knot must succeed on a Strength or Instinct Reaction Roll or take 1d12 direct magic damage and start a Death Countdown (4). All countdowns tick down when any PC within the scene rolls with Fear. When it triggers, the target makes a death move. If the Mandrake Knot is defeated, all Death Countdowns end.

name: Revel Lord
description: A faun dressed in bright clothes with a shepherd's crook tied with an amphora of wine.

features.name: Party Foul
features.description: When the Revel Lord marks HP, all creatures within Very Close range mark a Stress.

features.name: Drunken Haze
features.description: Mark a Stress to choose a point within Far range. A Very Close area around that point swirls with a faint mist. All targets within the area Swoon. Swooning targets are Vulnerable and have disadvantage on their next action roll, then clear the condition.

features.name: Cheers!
features.description: When an ally misses an attack within Very Close range of the Revel Lord, mark a Stress to allow them to reroll the attack.
features.trigger: ally misses attack within Very Close range

name: Dollmaker Hag
description: A hunched-over faerie with large, chitinous clawed hands and a terrifying grin with rows of sharp teeth.

features.name: Disturbing Presence
features.description: PCs that roll with Fear within Close range of the Hag must mark a Stress.

features.name: Stitch You Right Up
features.description: When the Hag is within Melee range of an ally, mark a Stress and the ally clears a HP.

features.name: Not This Time
features.description: When the hag takes Major or greater damage, mark a Stress to reduce the damage by 1d8.

features.name: How Dreadful! (Phase Change)
features.description: When the Dollmaker marks their last HP, replace them with the Puppetmaster Hag and immediately spotlight them.

name: Puppetmaster Hag
description: A tall faerie with massive claws and multiple smaller arms used to control the strings of her minions.

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.description: The Hag can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Let's Play
features.description: Twice per scene, spend a Fear to summon a Living Doll Horde, which appears within Close range.

features.name: Dance, Little Puppet!
features.description: Spend a Fear and choose a target within Far range to make an Instinct Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target begins to Dance. While Dancing the target is Vulnerable and cannot make action rolls other than to break free with a successful Strength or Presence Roll. If they fail to break free, the target marks 2 Stress.

features.name: Snip Snip
features.description: Mark a Stress to make an attack against all targets in Very Close range. Targets the Hag succeeds against take 2d6+3 magic damage and their hope die is reduced to a d10 until they roll with Hope.

features.name: Strawman Defense
features.description: When the Hag would take Major or greater damage from an attack, mark a Stress to cause a Living Doll Horde within Close range to mark a HP and reduce the number of HP marked by 1.

features.name: Incompetence Is Contagious
features.description: When a PC rolls a Failure with Fear within Close range of the Hag, all PCs within Far range lose a Hope.
features.trigger: PC rolls Failure with Fear within Close range

name: Living Doll Horde
description: A small, broken-looking doll with features that resemble humanoids with an unswerving loyalty to their creator.

features.name: Horde (2d4+3)
features.description: When the Horde has marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals 2d4+3 physical damage instead.

features.name: Empty Vessel
features.description: Once per scene, when the Horde makes a successful attack, mark 1 HP and summon a Totem of Fear. Describe how the doll takes on traits of the target.

name: Totem of Fear
description: A small, broken-looking doll with features that resemble humanoids and an unswerving loyalty to their creator.
motives: take over the life of the doppelganger

features.name: A Terrible Likeness
features.description: When the Totem of Fear enters the scene, designate the PC that they appear to look like as the doppelganger. When the Totem of Fear attacks the doppelganger, they have advantage on their attacks.

name: Hakturi
description: A doe-eyed creature of the wood in desperate need of your help.

features.name: Leech
features.description: When a PC encounters a Hakturi for the first time, all PCs within Close range gain a Hope. If PCs complete a long rest within Close range of a Hakturi, they must mark 1d4 Stress and their Hope die becomes a d10 until the Hakturi is defeated or the PCs rest again outside of its presence. For each Stress the PCs mark while in Close range of the Hakturi, the Hakturi gains a friend token.

features.name: Romp
features.description: Make a standard attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, deal 2d4+2 physical damage and the target must succeed on a Presence Reaction Roll or become temporarily Vulnerable. If the target fails, you can spend a friend token to immediately spotlight another Hakturi within Close range.

features.name: Empathy
features.description: When a befriended Hakturi takes damage from a once friendly PC, spend a Fear and all targets within Close range must succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target must mark 2 Stress and their next attack is made at disadvantage.

features.name: Evolve (Phase Change)
features.description: When the Hakturi accumulates 12 friend tokens, replace them with the Hakturi Mother and immediately spotlight them.

name: Hakturi Mother
description: A beachball-sized fluffy forest creature that uses its adorable nature to subdue prey.

features.name: Hypnotic Pollen
features.description: Spend a Fear to force all targets within Very Close range to succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll (15). Targets that fail are Infatuated and see all Hakturi as allies. A target that is Infatuated must mark a Stress if any Hakturi marks HP within Far range of them. This effect lasts until the target succeeds on a Presence or Knowledge Roll. PCs who have been Infatuated during the scene have disadvantage on action rolls that target the Hakturi Mother.

features.name: Overwhelming Odds
features.description: Mark a Stress to spotlight a number of Hakturi equal to the Hakturi Mother's unmarked HP.

features.name: Pack of Lies
features.description: Twice per scene, spend a Fear to summon 2 Hakturi that appear within Close range.

features.name: Demure Disposition
features.description: When a target that has disadvantage fails a roll against the Hakturi Mother, they mark a Stress and you gain a Fear.

name: Plurodin
description: A highly intelligent, human-sized amphibian with multiple dextrous appendages and folds of skin which act as pockets.

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.description: The Plurodin can be spotlighted up to 2 times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Slimy
features.description: The Plurodin's skin is coated in slippery mucus preventing it from being Restrained.

features.name: Chugalug
features.description: Mark a Stress to drink one of the potions the Plurodin has stashed on their body. Mark it as used on the "Breaking Things" table below.

features.name: Breaking Things
features.description: When the Plurodin takes Major damage, one of the potion vials tucked in its skin pockets breaks. Roll a d12. The Plurodin is affected by the potion based on a table (Minor Health Potion clears 1 HP, Minor Stamina Potion clears 1 Stress, Stride Potion allows Far movement, Bolster Potion adds extra die, Control Potion gives +1 Difficulty, Attune Potion gives Opportunity Attack Reaction, Charm Potion clears a condition). Once all instances of a potion have been used, that result becomes null going forward.

name: Hulking Bodyguard
description: A giant, muscled creature who is paid handsomely to protect their client.

features.name: You Da Boss
features.description: When the Bodyguard enters the scene, designate one adversary to be their boss.

features.name: Outta My Way!
features.description: Spend a Fear to make an attack against all targets within Close range. Targets the Bodyguard succeeds against take 2d12+6 physical damage and are knocked back to Far range.

features.name: I Got You, Boss!
features.description: When the Bodyguard's boss marks HP from an attacker in Close range of the Bodyguard, mark a Stress to move into Very Close range of the attacker and make a standard attack against them.
features.trigger: boss marks HP from attacker in Close range

features.name: Intimidating Presence
features.description: When a PC attacks the Bodyguard and misses, they lose a Hope.
